skill.name: micro-skill-creator
skill.description: Creates focused AI skills with specialist-agent prompts, explicit contracts, validation checklists, and workflow composition guidance.
skill.summary: Build atomic AI skills with structured prompts, contracts, and validation plans.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 54 issues, but manual review found false positives in Markdown and YAML examples. No executable code, network calls, secret access, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration pattern was found. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because it is community content and contains broad prompt templates.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The 37 external command alerts point to fenced Markdown or YAML examples in SKILL.md. They are instructional templates, not executable Ruby, shell, or backtick evaluation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited external command location is inside a fenced example or nearby template prose. The repository contains only SKILL.md and no runnable script.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alerts in Prompting Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts occur in natural language headings, descriptions, YAML placeholders, or template examples. No code imports or calls cryptographic APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, signing, or password handling code. The alerts are tied to prose and placeholders, not cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Alerts in Examples
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance at line 157 is a testing checklist item. Network reconnaissance at line 394 names command integration, not discovery or scanning behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both cited lines are descriptive skill-design text. No network scanner, host discovery command, or environment enumeration instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Reusable AI workflows often become too broad to test or compose reliably. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create focused skills with clear contracts and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Defines one clear responsibility for a new skill.
Designs specialist-agent prompts for extraction, validation, generation, and analysis tasks.
Creates input and output contracts for composable workflows.
Adds validation checklists and test cases for normal, boundary, and error cases.
Describes ways to connect skills with cascades and slash commands.
Guides selection of evidence-based prompting patterns for different task types.
limitations: It provides skill design guidance, not a full automated packaging tool.
Generated skills still need human review before publication.
It does not verify external dependencies or marketplace policy compliance.
Neural training references require separate tooling that is not included.

use_cases.title: Create a Reusable Task Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated team workflow into one focused skill with a clear purpose and expected output.

use_cases.title: Design Specialist Agent Prompts
use_cases.description: Draft a domain-specific agent prompt with methodology, failure modes, and quality checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Skills for Workflow Composition
use_cases.description: Define contracts so several small skills can work together in a larger cascade.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Micro-Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one repeated task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a micro-skill for one repeated task. Define its purpose, trigger terms, input needs, output format, and basic tests.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Specialist Agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for stronger domain behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a specialist agent for this micro-skill. Include expertise, methodology, failure modes, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Composable Skill Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a micro-skill contract that can compose with other skills. Specify inputs, outputs, error handling, dependencies, and versioning concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Improve a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill draft for single responsibility, prompt clarity, validation coverage, integration points, and marketplace readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a micro-skill that validates API responses against an OpenAPI specification.
output_examples.output: A focused validation skill brief with purpose, specialist-agent behavior, accepted inputs, validation result output, error handling, and test scenarios.

output_examples.input: Design a micro-skill for extracting invoice fields from uploaded documents.
output_examples.output: A data extraction skill plan with confidence scoring, ambiguity handling, target fields, required source details, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: Improve this skill so it works inside a multi-step workflow.
output_examples.output: A revised skill outline with stable interfaces, dependency notes, failure states, and handoff expectations for adjacent skills.

best_practices: Keep each skill limited to one responsibility and one clear trigger pattern.
Define explicit input, output, validation, and failure handling sections.
Review generated skills with realistic normal, boundary, and error cases.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated tasks into one broad skill.
Do not publish generated skills without human review and testing.
Do not rely on vague output expectations when another skill must consume the result.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured designs for small AI skills with specialist prompts, contracts, tests, and integration notes.

faq.question: Does it generate complete marketplace packages?
faq.answer: It focuses on skill design and documentation. Packaging, policy checks, and publication steps need separate review.

faq.question: Can it help with Claude Code workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes trigger patterns, slash command bindings, and composable workflow contracts for Claude Code use.

faq.question: Is it only for developers?
faq.answer: No. It can help any user convert repeatable AI work into a clear, testable skill design.

faq.question: How should generated skills be tested?
faq.answer: Test normal inputs, invalid inputs, edge cases, output format compliance, and integration with adjacent workflow steps.

faq.question: Does it run external commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed file contains Markdown and YAML examples, not executable command code.
